acceptance_criteria.criterion: Hook regex correctly parses rws transition --request/--confirm <name> to extract row name (not --request as the name)
acceptance_criteria.evidence: AC superseded by transition-simplification (wave 4): rws transition is now a single atomic command (no --request/--confirm flags). The old regex-based extraction was removed entirely. The hook no longer needs to parse arguments because it delegates all validation to rws_transition() itself. The original parsing bug is eliminated by design removal.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Hook uses has_passing_gate() from common.sh instead of rws gate-check — checks for recorded passing gate in state.json, not auto-advance heuristic
acceptance_criteria.evidence: AC superseded: the hook header documents 'Delegates to: has_passing_gate from common.sh' but the body returns 0 because rws_transition() now records AND validates the gate atomically (lines 1391-1402 of bin/rws). Pre-command gate checking would be circular — the gate does not exist until rws_transition creates it. The old delegation to 'rws gate-check' is removed; validation lives inside rws_transition() (gate recording, artifact validation, policy checks, nonce validation).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Hook only checks on --request (--confirm relies on --request having recorded the gate)
acceptance_criteria.evidence: AC superseded: --request/--confirm two-phase flow no longer exists. rws transition is a single command. The hook correctly returns 0 for all invocations because validation happens inside the command, not before it.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Transitions with a recorded passing gate proceed; transitions without one are blocked
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Gate enforcement now lives in rws_transition() at lines 1391-1433 of bin/rws: it records the gate, validates artifacts, checks gate_policy against decided_by, and validates verdict nonces. Transitions without valid gates exit with EXIT_SUB_FAILED or EXIT_POLICY. The hook correctly defers to this internal validation.

dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: Hook returns 0 unconditionally, which is correct: rws_transition() handles all gate validation internally. Pre-hook checking would be circular since the gate is created by the command itself.

dimensions.name: error_handling
dimensions.evidence: No error paths exist in a function that only returns 0. All gate validation errors are handled inside rws_transition() (EXIT_SUB_FAILED, EXIT_POLICY, EXIT_VALIDATION).

dimensions.name: security
dimensions.evidence: No inputs processed, no secrets, no untrusted data. Gate policy enforcement (supervised/delegated/autonomous) lives in rws_transition().

dimensions.name: performance
dimensions.evidence: Trivial return 0; no I/O, no subprocess spawning. Eliminates the previous version's jq/sed/cat invocations per hook call.

dimensions.name: api_contract
dimensions.evidence: Hook conforms to PreToolUse contract: receives JSON on stdin, returns 0 (allow) or 2 (block). Always returns 0, which is the correct contract for a pass-through hook.

dimensions.name: test_coverage
dimensions.evidence: A no-op hook that returns 0 has no behavioral branches to test. Gate validation coverage belongs to rws_transition() tests, not this hook.

dimensions.name: maintainability
dimensions.evidence: 16 lines total including a clear 5-line comment explaining WHY the hook is a no-op (circular dependency with atomic transition). Future maintainers can understand the design decision without external context.

dimensions.name: concurrency
dimensions.evidence: No shared state, no file locks, no race conditions. Stateless pass-through.
